Read Research Get an Audit
Free · Every Tuesday

The Weekly
Growth Report

One thing every Tuesday, a specific tactic, benchmark, or finding from companies doing $1M–$5M in your trade. What they spend on ads, how they answer calls, what their close rate looks like. Short. Useful. Written for owners who'd rather know their numbers than guess.

Unsubscribe anytime. We don't share your email.

📊
Real benchmarks from real companies
Not industry surveys or guesswork. Actual data from analyzing hundreds of HVAC, roofing, and plumbing companies: answer rates, cost per booked job, close rates, Google Ad spend, by trade and market size.
🔧
One specific thing you can use this week
Each issue is built around one tactic, finding, or system. Not a 20-minute read. Not a vague strategy framework. Something you can hand to your front desk, show to your agency, or implement yourself in a few hours.
📋
Contractor-first language
We write like a contractor who figured out growth systems, not a marketer. You'll see "calls" not "leads," "book jobs" not "convert," "average ticket" not "AOV." If it won't pass the 6am truck test, we don't publish it.
Recent Issues

What you get
every Tuesday

Each issue covers one topic in depth. Specific numbers, specific actions, specific dollar amounts. Here's what's gone out recently.

Issue #03
Google Ads · HVAC
The $4,200/Month Google Ads Mistake 44% of HVAC Companies Make

Nearly half of HVAC companies spending on Google Ads have zero conversion tracking installed - which means zero visibility into which campaigns are producing booked jobs vs. burning cash. Here's what it costs, and the 15-minute fix any owner can do without touching the campaign settings.

44% have zero tracking 15-min fix Avg $2,800/mo wasted
Issue #02
Close Rate · All Trades
Your Close Rate Isn't 35%. It's Probably 22%.

Most owners overestimate their close rate by 10–15 percentage points because they're tracking the wrong thing. They count "submitted estimates" instead of "answered calls." Here's how to find the real number - and what the top 25% of companies in your trade actually close at.

Industry avg: 22–28% Top 25%: 38–45%
Issue #01
Estimate Follow-Up · All Trades
The Follow-Up Sequence That Recovered $47K in Lost Estimates

68% of estimates sent to homeowners never receive a follow-up call. One roofing company changed that with a 3-touch text and email sequence - and recovered $47,000 in jobs that had gone cold. Here's the exact sequence, word for word.

68% never followed up $47K recovered 3-touch sequence

this Tuesday.

We don't share your email. No spam.
